title: Lasthenia gracilis
taxonomy: Lasthenia gracilis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Madieae, a group of plants that are commonly found in dry to moderately moist habitats. The genus Lasthenia is a group of plants that are native to western North America, and are often referred to as "goldfields." Lasthenia gracilis is a perennial herb that typically grows from 10 to 30 centimeters in height, and produces small yellow flowers in the summer months. It is found in a variety of habitats, including meadows, grasslands, and rocky outcrops.
uses: Lasthenia gracilis, also known as San Diego goldfields, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly by the indigenous peoples of the region. benefits: Lasthenia gracilis, also known as slender goldfields, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits.
